title: nc -p considered harmful
body:
nmap-ncat has been downgraded on Fedora, to 7.92.
nc -l -p PORT requires 7.95. Switch to nc -l ADDR PORT.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Sigh.... [no joy](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5448450249064448/html/int-podman-fedora-40-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-networking-podman-run-network-bind-to-127-0-0-1--1). Giving up for the day.
We can do this change but I don't think this is actually the problem.

I understand it now, nmap-ncat got downgraded to licensing issues: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2296006
And the older version does not work with `-p` as we expected as you have shown above. Therefore this change is correct and should be merged.

I don't think it is related the the int flake (#23263) as this would be consistent problem.
LGTM but remove the fixes please
Thanks for the investigation. I updated the commit and commit message.
